premises.statement: ChemSpec represents the alkali-metal fragment as an M+ site core with one domain-owned delocalized valence electron for explanatory execution.
ChemSpec represents water as neutral molecular H2O with two localized O-H single bonds.
ChemSpec represents each supported alkali-metal hydroxide as an ionic association of M+ and covalently bonded OH- components.
ChemSpec represents hydrogen gas as neutral molecular H2 with one localized H-H single bond.
Contact between Li, Na, K, Rb, or Cs metal and water has the reviewed representative outcome 2 M + 2 H2O -> 2 MOH + H2.
The listed Li, Na, K, Ca, H, and O tuples are ChemSpec's closed explanatory execution domain for this family and its unsupported probe; they are not a claim of physical mechanism.
Hydrogen is compatible with the authored observation predicate evolves for this rule outcome.
The selected alkali metal is compatible with the authored observation predicate disappears for this family outcome.

The 118 candidate records use IUPAC element symbols, names, atomic numbers, and periods; optional group values and conventional block assignments are candidate interpretations covered by the host-selected AI review.

The recorded ionic charges, water-reaction classes, and separate metal, hydrogen, and halogen displacement ranks are reviewed closed-world screening facts; a rank is comparable only within its named activity series.

applicability.required_context: representative educational outcome under the reviewed standard-outcome premise
